title: Benstonea atrocarpa
taxonomy: Benstonea atrocarpa is a plant species that belongs to the family Pandanaceae. The classification of Benstonea atrocarpa places it within the genus Benstonea. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Australia, specifically in Western Australia. Its classification further categorizes it as a monocot, which is a group of flowering plants with a single cotyledon in their seeds. As part of the Pandanaceae family, Benstonea atrocarpa shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as Pandanus.

intro: Benstonea atrocarpa, also known as "black benstonea," is a species of flowering plant in the family Pandanaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory infections, and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Benstonea atrocarpa include lignans, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as benzoic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies for its purported health benefits.
